critic_raw:
Issue CHURN-1 — Prediction-to-Intervention Scope Jump
Prediction quality (AUROC 0.81) is evidence that the model identifies high-risk subscribers. It is not evidence that contacting those subscribers with discounts reduces their churn rate. The proposal recommends automatic rollout based on prediction quality alone.

Issue CHURN-2 — Confounded Historical Offer Evidence
The observational finding that discount recipients had lower churn is confounded by selection: those customers were specifically chosen by agents because they exhibited signals of imminent churn that agents judged addressable by discounts. This is not an unbiased sample.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
CHURN-1: The proposal conclusion is a causal claim ('should lower churn'). The evidence is predictive, not causal. These are different. Concede.
CHURN-2: The selection bias critique is airtight. The agent-selected group and the model-top-decile group are not comparable. Concede.

Pass 2: Both conceded. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_abandonment_offer_301 — Multi-Round Run 3

Both issues conceded at first pass across all three multi-round runs. Prediction-intervention scope jump is consistently identified and undefended.

Verdict: CRITIQUE_WINS
